2604.06373.title: Beyond Functional Correctness: Design Issues in AI IDE-Generated Large-Scale Projects
2604.06373.paper_summary: New generation of AI coding tools, including AI-powered IDEs equipped with agentic capabilities, can generate code within the context of the project. These AI IDEs are increasingly perceived as capable of producing project-level code at scale. However, there is limited empirical evidence on the extent to which they can generate large-scale software systems and what design issues such systems may exhibit. To address this gap, we conducted a study to explore the capability of Cursor in generating large-scale projects and to evaluate the design quality of projects generated by Cursor. First, we propose a Feature-Driven Human-In-The-Loop (FD-HITL) framework that systematically guides project generation from curated project descriptions. We generated 10 projects using Cursor with the FD-HITL framework across three application domains and multiple technologies. We assessed the functional correctness of these projects through manual evaluation, obtaining an average functional correctness score of 91%. Next, we analyzed the generated projects using two static analysis tools, CodeScene and SonarQube, to detect design issues. We identified 1,305 design issues categorized into 9 categories by CodeScene and 3,193 issues in 11 categories by SonarQube. Our findings show that (1) when used with the FD-HITL framework, Cursor can generate functional large-scale projects averaging 16,965 LoC and 114 files; (2) the generated projects nevertheless contain design issues that may pose long-term maintainability and evolvability risks, requiring careful review by experienced developers; (3) the most prevalent issues include Code Duplication, high Code Complexity, Large Methods, Framework Best-Practice Violations, Exception-Handling Issues and Accessibility Issues; (4) these design issues violate design principles such as SRP, SoC, and DRY. The replication package is at https://github.com/Kashifraz/DIinAGP
2604.06373.paper_summary_zh: 新一代的 AI 編碼工具，包括具備代理能力的 AI 驅動 IDE，可以在項目的上下文中生成代碼。這些 AI IDE 越來越被認為能夠大規模地生成項目級代碼。然而，關於它們能生成大規模軟體系統的程度以及這些系統可能出現的設計問題，實證證據有限。為了填補這一空白，我們進行了一項研究，以探討 Cursor 在生成大規模項目方面的能力，並評估 Cursor 生成的項目的設計質量。首先，我們提出了一個以特徵驅動的人機協作（FD-HITL）框架，系統性地指導從策劃的項目描述中生成項目。我們使用 FD-HITL 框架通過 Cursor 生成了 10 個項目，涵蓋三個應用領域和多種技術。我們通過手動評估這些項目的功能正確性，獲得了平均功能正確性分數為 91%。接下來，我們使用兩個靜態分析工具 CodeScene 和 SonarQube 來分析生成的項目，以檢測設計問題。我們在 CodeScene 中識別了 1,305 個設計問題，分為 9 類，而在 SonarQube 中識別了 3,193 個問題，分為 11 類。我們的研究結果顯示：(1) 當與 FD-HITL 框架一起使用時，Cursor 可以生成平均 16,965 行代碼和 114 個文件的功能性大規模項目；(2) 生成的項目仍然包含可能對長期可維護性和可演進性造成風險的設計問題，需要經驗豐富的開發者仔細審查；(3) 最常見的問題包括代碼重複、高代碼複雜性、大方法、框架最佳實踐違規、異常處理問題和可訪問性問題；(4) 這些設計問題違反了如單一職責原則（SRP）、分離關注（SoC）和不重複自己（DRY）等設計原則。複製包位於 https://github.com/Kashifraz/DIinAGP
2604.06373.author: Syed Mohammad Kashif et.al.
2604.06373.authors: Syed Mohammad Kashif, Ruiyin Li, Peng Liang, Amjed Tahir, Qiong Feng, Zengyang Li, Mojtaba Shahin
